decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses simulations across 30 scenarios to benchmark the accuracy, precision, bias, and robustness of more than 20 MDRI estimators.

decisions.relationship_evidence: A simulation study demonstrates how tree-prior misspecification biases inferred epidemic clock rates.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Tests a proposed inference framework in four simulation studies and assesses reconstruction accuracy, robustness, and uncertainty.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Simulates vaccine trials and compares seven VES estimators with respect to accuracy and bias under limited ascertainment.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses simulations to compare parameter-estimation bias, uncertainty, confidence intervals, and mean squared error under overdispersion.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Simulates outbreak scenarios to compare logistic- and robust Poisson-regression risk estimates and source identification.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses simulated outbreaks to compare trial designs and analysis methods by power, sensitivity to weighting, and other statistical properties.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Empirically benchmarks immunoassay sensitivity and specificity on blood samples without a simulation study of estimator behavior.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses simulated data to quantify bias in R0 and Rt estimates caused by changing diagnostic testing and reporting.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Simulates infection spread and intervention-related growth regimes rather than evaluating estimator operating characteristics.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Evaluates four basic-reproduction-number estimators using eight impartial simulated scenarios and discusses their biases, advantages, and flaws.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses simulated Ebola outbreak data to compare machine-learning imputation performance and resulting CFR bias and precision.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit simulation evaluation of estimator performance under missingness.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Derives and cross-validates prediction models using observed hospital data rather than simulated estimator operating characteristics.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Simulates incomplete case detection and compares relative bias across four epidemic measures.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Simulates adaptive platform and stand-alone trials and compares development time, sample size, and statistical operating characteristics.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Validates a simulation-based Rt estimator on synthetic temporally aggregated incidence data and demonstrates estimation accuracy.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses extensive simulations to evaluate sample size, familywise type I error, and power under alternative MAMS selection rules.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses synthetic surveillance and vaccination data to quantify vaccine-effectiveness estimator bias under varied underreporting scenarios.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses analytical results and stochastic simulations to evaluate parameter identifiability under prior immunity and underreporting.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses simulated gonorrhea spread to evaluate accuracy and bias of phylodynamic sexual-network bridging estimators across sampling schemes.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a mechanistic differential-equation model of within-patient biomarkers and clinical progression, not a compartmental transmission model.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a stochastic mechanistic model of MRSA transmission through transient contamination and persistent colonization states.
